title: (Senior) Program & Project Manager Business Operations (m/f/d)
description:
Everyone's story matters. Come shape your story with us at Riverty.
   

  

**But where does that take you?**
 To one of our 30 hybrid workspaces – designed for exchanging ideas, learning from others, and shaping the way we work. An international community of over 4,000 people, representing almost 80 nationalities across 11 countries. United by one mission: Combining empathy, advanced technology and data\\-driven insights to keep people and businesses in flow. With payments made for them. So that they don't have to worry about it.
   

  

**And there's more:** 
 We are part of the family\\-owned Bertelsmann group. Established. Corporate. In a fast\\-paced industry. We enable flexible payments in various industries, simplifying the financial management of known brands and helping people repay debt to build financial confidence. In short: shaping FinTech.
   

  

**We are looking for a**
**(Senior) Program \\& Project Manager – Business Operations (m/f/d)**
**(unlimited, full\\-time) to join our team at one of our locations in Berlin, Verl or Münster and help shape the next generation of fintech \\- flexible working conditions available**
 In this role, you’ll drive clear prioritization and alignment of initiatives, monitor implementation progress and KPI achievement, and ensure transparency for all relevant stakeholders. In addition to this central responsibility, you’ll independently manage and lead individual projects.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
* Oversee multiple efficiency and AI initiatives across various countries to promote operational excellence and deliver measurable business outcomes.
* Ensure transparency and alignment among stakeholders through structured governance, prioritization, and clear communication.
* Monitor and report on progress and performance, interpret key KPIs and analytics to ensure goal achievement and data\\-driven decision\\-making.
* Share best practices and insights across countries and functions to enhance collaboration and efficiency.
* Take ownership of selected initiatives or projects and ensure successful execution using professional project management methodologies.


**Qualifications:**
* University degree or equivalent qualification (preferably in Business Administration, Finance, or Technology).
* 3–5 years of experience in project and/or program management, ideally with strong knowledge of financial services and experience working with customer service and operations teams.
* Experience in receivables management is a strong plus.
* Solid understanding of project management methodologies, with experience in lateral leadership and stakeholder management.
* Proven track record in leading data\\-driven and AI projects across functions and countries.
* Ability to present complex information to diverse audiences and manage multiple ongoing efficiency initiatives.
* Preferred: Experience with Jira; certification or hands\\-on experience in Lean Management, Six Sigma, Kaizen, or similar methodologies.
* Languages: Fluent in German and English.


 Equal Opportunity Employer Statement
   

  

 We want to be a fair and inclusive employer. We value the diverse perspectives that a diverse workforce brings to the table. Therefore, we are actively looking for people who enrich our company through their identity, background and personal experiences, with or without a disability.
   

  

**Benefits:**
* At Riverty, you can be who you are. We are committed to creating an inclusive environment and a culture of appreciation, enriched by our employee networks.
* Prioritize your health with supported sports and leisure activities.
* Take advantage of our numerous training and development opportunities! Enhance your skills with training offered by the Bertelsmann University, language courses, or leadership training.
* Benefit from our discounts on Bertelsmann products and financial incentives.
* With our diverse work models, you can tailor your work to your preferences. Take advantage of mobile office, flexible working hours, and part\\-time models.

title: Project Manager
description:
At Westinghouse Electric Company, we are pioneers in nuclear technology and our technology is the basis for nearly half of the world's commercial nuclear power plants in operation. Today, we are also building several new reactors that contribute to more fossil\\-free energy. Globally, we have over 10,000 employees in 19 countries, of which 1,000 are at our Swedish workplaces in Västerås and Täby. Our Swedish operations were founded in 1969 and supply nuclear fuel as well as products and services in automation, maintenance, testing and decommissioning for customers around the world. Read more about us www.westinghousenuclear.com .
   

  

 Project Manager – Environmental Solutions (D\\&D Projects)
   

  

**Westinghouse Electric Company** 
**Mannheim**
**Welcome to the future of nuclear energy** 
 , where Westinghouse Electric Company is leading the field with expertise and innovation to shape the power of tomorrow.
   

  

 At Westinghouse, innovation is in our DNA. We are creative. We think differently. We reimagine the possible across the nuclear industry every day.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Project Manager you will lead major D\\&D projects in Germany, with budgets ranging from €25M to €120M. This position involves managing cross\\-functional teams of 10–30 professionals, including technical experts, site operations, and back\\-office functions (project controls, administration, procurement). You will ensure projects are delivered safely, on time, and within budget in a highly regulated environment.
   

  

 You will report to the Project Director and be located at Mannheim.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Lead all aspects of project execution: scope, schedule, cost, risk, procurement, quality, resources, and stakeholder engagement.
* Develop and maintain project management plans, baselines, and risk registers.
* Manage multidisciplinary teams in a matrix environment; foster collaboration and accountability.
* Oversee financial performance, forecasting, and reporting.
* Maintain strong relationships with clients, regulators, and internal stakeholders.
* Ensure compliance with safety, environmental, and regulatory requirements.
* Drive continuous improvement and lessons learned.


**Qualifications:**
* Education: Bachelor’s degree in Project Management, Engineering, Business, or related field. Preferred: Advanced degree.
* Experience: 5–10 years of project management experience in technical industries; proven track record managing large, complex projects (€25M–€120M).
* Certifications: PMI PMP or equivalent preferred.
* Languages: Fluent English and German required; Spanish, French, or Italian is a plus.
* Technical Skills: Advanced knowledge of scheduling and cost management tools (Primavera/MSP, SAP); proficiency in MS Office and data visualization tools; familiarity with risk management tools.
* Soft Skills: Strong leadership, strategic thinking, communication, and negotiation abilities.


**Why Westinghouse?**
 We know that to put forth your best effort, you need to be challenged and enjoy what you do in a supportive and respectful environment. We aim to maintain this balance by offering our employees the amenities, benefits and training they need to reach personal and professional goals.
   

  

**Below is an example of what employees in Germany can expect:** 
  

  

* Great benefits for your convenience and safety
+ Employee Assistance Program: confidential counseling and resources for employees, eligible dependents, and household members
+ Peer\\-to\\-peer recognition program.
+ Learning and development opportunities.

* We encourage our employees to participate in community service events and other team\\-building activities to strengthen their bonds and inspire each other.


**You can learn more about Westinghouse by visiting** 
 http://www.westinghousenuclear.com .
   

  

 Westinghouse is an Equal Opportunity Employer including Veterans and Individuals with Disabilities.

 At Westinghouse, we offer an inspiring work environment in a high\\-tech industry. You will be a part of a network of global experts from several different areas and we offer great opportunities for competence development and career growth, both in Sweden and abroad. As a member of our team, you will work together with engaged colleagues and be a part of an environment that is both challenging and supportive. Many of our positions are based in activity\\-based environments which enables a flexible workplace.
   

  

 At Westinghouse, we have a strong safety culture.
   

  

 Westinghouse is an employer that treats everyone equally.

title: Project Manager (Germany)
description:
The Project Manager ensures the successful initiation, planning, execution, monitoring, controlling and closure of assigned clinical research projects. The Project Manager I must ensure compliance with the study budget, project scope and timelines and in accordance with applicable standard operating procedures (SOPs), good clinical practices, regulatory and study\\-specific requirements. These tasks are done under close supervision by Senior Project Manager/Program Director and/or Associate Director, Project Management
   

  

**This role will be perfect for you if:**
* We can count on you to deliver results while using a disciplined approach to project management
* You are looking for the next career step to prepare you to become a project manager.
* You are knowledgeable about clinical research projects and looking to continue learning.
* Working in an organization that is driven by science and innovation and completing meaningful work is important to you.


**Responsibilities**
* May serve as primary contact for the Sponsor, vendors and internal team throughout the study.
* Coordinates tasks and deadlines between the different departments involved in the project.
* Oversees project coordinators, SSU team members, RDA and RAC resources to ensure correct prioritization of site activation activities amongst team members
* Interacts with vendor management to ensure vendor supplies and services are coordinated for with site activation timelines
* Escalates to project manager II when site activation timelines are at risk or cannot be maintained.
* Assists with managing the needs and expectations of the Sponsor and other internal and external project stakeholders.
* Assists with ensuring all team members are adequately trained on the project.
* May plan the activities and resources (e.g. internal and external resources, equipment, etc.) required for the project.
* Manages the quality of assigned work and deliverables
* Assists with providing project status updates to external and internal stakeholders
* Ensures assigned tasks are completed in compliance with the study budget, project scope and timelines and in accordance with applicable standard operating procedures (SOPs), good clinical practices, regulatory and study\\-specific requirements.
* Reconciles study trackers
* Assists with analyzing discrepancies between planned and actual results and participates in the development and implementation of corrective actions to be taken as needed.
* Assists with enforcing effective change control and risk management throughout the project.
* Reviews and may assist in the drafting of project operational plans, processes, and manuals as applicable (e.g., project management plan, monitoring plan, etc.).
* Assists with ensuring that study specific documents and project deliverables (e.g., protocol, informed consent form, electronic case report form (eCRF), tables/listings/figures (TLFs), clinical study report, etc.) meet applicable country requirements.
* Oversee activities related to sites selection (feasibility questionnaires, sites selection, planning of site qualification visits)
* Monitors patient recruitment, subject status, and follows up with sites on recruitment strategy plan.
* Participates in the planning and conduct of Investigator’s Meeting.
* In collaboration with the Regulatory Affairs group, may oversee activities related to central ethics and regulatory submissions.
* Ensures collection of required essential documents from the sites prior to study initiation and maintains of currency of site level documentation throughout the study.
* May assist the project manager II with quality reviews and or audits of the Trial Master File (TMF) to ensure inspection readiness.
* May support clinical monitoring activities, such as CRA training, visit report review, site letters, and escalated site issues
* May track site qualification, initiation, routine and close\\-out visits, project\\-specific training, monitoring visit reports and follow\\-up letters, compliance with monitoring plan, escalation of site\\-related issues.
* Maintains the project specific training matrix and confirms project team members are fully training per the study matrix prior to team members performing study tasks
* Supports the sites and ensures that each site has the necessary material to adequately perform the study (e.g., investigational product, study supplies, special equipment, safety lab kits, etc.).
* In collaboration with the Data Management group, may ensure that the CRF complies with the protocol and Sponsor requirements and ensures queries resolution and data review process follow the study timelines until database lock.
* May provide technical, therapeutic and project management expertise in training and process improvement efforts for the department.


 Requirements:
   

  

**IDEAL PROFILE**
**Education**
* B.Sc. in a related field of study to clinical research


**Experience**
* At least 3 years of experience in a similar role, including activities related to management of clinical studies in the pharmaceutical, biotechnology and/or CRO industry
* At least 2 years experience coordinating activities related clinical trial management


**Knowledge And Skills**
* Excellent knowledge of GCP and ICH standards, FDA and Canadian regulations
* Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Office suite
* Excellent oral and written skills in English, French is an asset
* Excellent communication skills
* Ability to work in a team environment and establish good relationships with colleagues and sponsors
* Good problem\\-solving abilities
* Good organizational skills
* Strong ability to carry out different projects and work under pressure while meeting timelines
* Experience managing small to medium projects using a disciplined approach to project management
* Core Project Management competencies
* Demonstrated ability to establish and deliver resource\\-based project plans
* Excellent people management skills; experience working with and managing teams in a matrix environment


 Our company:
   

  

**The work environment**
**At Indero, you will work with brilliant and driven colleagues. Our values are collaboration, innovation, reliability, and responsiveness. We offer a stimulating work environment and attractive advancement opportunities.**
**Work location**
**Note that this opening is for a home\\-based position in Germany**
**About Indero**
**Indero is a contract research organization (CRO) specialized in dermatology. Since its beginnings in 2000, our organization has benefited from a solid reputation for the quality of its research and services exceeding the expectations of its clients. Based in Montreal, Indero continues to grow and expand in North America and Europe.**
*Indero is committed to providing equitable treatment and equal opportunity to all individuals. As such, Indero will provide accommodations throughout the recruitment and selection process to applicants with disabilities, upon request.*
*Indero only accepts applicants who can legally work in Germany.*

title: Project Manager
description:
Geek\\+ is a global technology company specialized in smart logistics. We apply advanced robotics and artificial intelligence technologies to create solutionsfor warehouse and factory operations.

Speed, product diversification, same\\-day delivery, volume, safety… these concerns are impacting a wide range of industries, from apparel, retail, automotive, to even electronics, energy and pharmaceutical industries.

title: Program Manager (all genders)
description:
**Your mission**
 At Impossible Founders, we're building Germany's boldest DeepTech Startup Factory. Our mission: to turn disruptive technologies into real businesses that change the world. We partner with top researchers, fearless entrepreneurs, and leading institutions to launch ventures in fields like AI, quantum tech, new materials, and Green Tech. If you're ready to build what others think is impossible — this is your place.
   

  

 In this early stage of Impossible Founders, you help shape the core of what we do: our entrepreneurship, incubation, and acceleration programs. You design, implement, and continuously improve the formats that support our founders along their DeepTech journey.
   

  

 You will own a specific focus area, like Green Tech, New Materials, or advanced engineering solutions, and within this focus area will coordinate internal processes, support startups personally, and make sure everything runs smoothly across all program activities. As we grow, you will continuously deepen our expertise and expand our network to support your focus area, with plenty of room to grow and take ownership.
   

  

**Program \\& Strategy Delivery**
* Own and grow our deeptech founder programs: design, plan, and run incubators, accelerators, bootcamps, workshops, and new experimental formats that move teams from idea to traction.
* Keep us ahead of the curve: benchmark our initiatives nationally and internationally, spot trends in deeptech, and turn them into actionable improvements that will enable our startups to succeed.


**Founder Success \\& Coaching**
* Be the go\\-to partner for founders: lead regular check\\-ins, run coaching sessions, connect the right mentors at the right moment, and create an environment where founders feel challenged and supported.
* Guide mentoring with intention: manage mentor matching and ensure mentoring relationships are productive, relevant, and outcome\\-driven.


**Ecosystem \\& Partnerships**
* Build an ecosystem in your focus area: develop and maintain partnerships with startups, experts, corporates, and investors, to unlock pilots, customers, capital, and top\\-tier expertise for our startups.


**Community, Events \\& Experience**
* Create moments that founders remember — organize high\\-energy events, workshops, and networking formats that spark collaborations, deal flow, and honest peer learning.


**Impact, Reporting \\& Stakeholdermanagement**
* Make impact visible and fundable — track progress, monitor outcomes, and translate results into crisp reports for management and funding bodies, showing what worked, what didn’t, and what’s next.


**Your profile**
 The role of Program Manager calls for someone who loves enabling founders to move forward. You understand what it takes to turn ideas into companies – through experience in startup or innovation programs, as an investor in DeepTech startups, or by working closely with founders. You navigate confidently between structure and flexibility and feel at home in the DeepTech and startup ecosystem.
   

  

 Because Impossible Founders is still in an early build\\-up phase, we’re looking for someone with a strong hands\\-on mentality. You like to take ownership, test formats, and iterate quickly based on feedback. You’re not afraid to get into the operational details and at the same time, you keep the overall founder journey in mind.
   

  

**What Matters Most To Us**
* Experience in at least one relevant DeepTech field — particularly Green Tech, New Materials, or advanced engineering solutions — ideally gained through work with startups, investors, research institutions, or innovation programs
* Solid understanding of entrepreneurship, innovation management, or tech\\-driven business models
* Strong organizational skills and a structured, reliable way of working – you keep track of many moving parts
* High ownership and a proactive mindset – you spot what’s needed and make it happen
* Genuine motivation to support founders and tech ideas, not just “run a program”
* Team\\-oriented, collaborative working style – you enjoy building things together rather than alone


**Additionally, it would be a plus if you bring**
* First experience as a founder, co\\-founder, or core team member in a startup
* Experience in working with public or private partners (e.g. universities, corporates, funding bodies)
* Familiarity with startup ecosystems, especially in DeepTech or research\\-based ventures
* Experience in moderating workshops, mentoring formats, or community\\-building activities


**Why us?**
 At Impossible Founders, the people behind the impossible are the most important catalyst. That's why we do everything we can to create a collaborative working environment:
   

  

* Responsibility \\& autonomy: You take on real responsibility from day one and have the freedom to actively shape your role and the company. Output counts, and you are given the freedom you need to do your job in the best possible way.
* Flexible hybrid working: We offer a high degree of flexibility through our hybrid work setup. Mondays are our shared office day, while on other days you can choose whether to work remotely or from the office — depending on what works best for your role, team, and personal circumstances.
* Meaningful mission \\& impact: You will work on projects that address real challenges in AI, climate tech, and beyond. You will achieve impact and visibility by working closely with founders, researchers, and leading partners.
* Collaborative culture \\& diversity: We cultivate a collaborative culture—practical, ego\\-free, and driven by shared responsibility. Our team is characterized by diversity and openness. The company language is English so that everyone feels welcome.


**Attractive working conditions:**
* 30 days of vacation
* Deutschlandticket
* Central office in a prime location
* Choice of your own hardware


**About Us**
**Impossible Founders** 
 is one of Germany’s boldest DeepTech startup factories. Our mission: turning groundbreaking technologies into real companies – with the potential to change the world. We collaborate with leading scientists, fearless founders, and renowned institutions. Our ventures emerge in future\\-focused fields like Artificial Intelligence, Quantum Technology, advanced materials, and Green Tech.
   

  

 If you’re ready to make the seemingly impossible possible, you’re in the right place.
   

  

 We welcome applications from all talented individuals, regardless of gender, age, sexual orientation, ethnicity, cultural background, or abilities. Diversity makes our team stronger!
   

  

 https://www.impossible\\-founders.com/

title: PMO Manager (Project Management Office) - Yoshi en (all genders)
description:
**Your role**
 We are looking for an experienced PMO Manager (Project Management Office) to support Yoshi en in planning, coordinating, and delivering our most important cross\\-functional projects.
   

  

 In this role, you will work closely with the Executive Management (CEO / COO / Executive Team) and department leads to create clear structures, processes, and transparency around how projects are prioritized and executed across the organization.
   

  

 This role offers strong visibility within the company and the opportunity to grow into a more strategic, executive\\-adjacent role, potentially evolving into a Chief of Staff position over time.
   

  

 What you’ll do
   

  

* Establish and maintain a company\\-wide project management framework (shared standards, tools, templates, and ways of working).
* Support Executive Management in prioritizing projects and aligning them with company strategy and available resources.
* Track and report on the overall project landscape (status, timelines, risks, budgets, and resource usage).
* Support and advise teams on project planning, risk management, and problem\\-solving.
* Ensure clear communication between project teams and Executive Management.
* Prepare structured updates, reports, and presentations for leadership decision\\-making.
* Lead selected complex or high\\-priority projects across multiple teams when needed.


**Your profile**
* A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Management, or a related field (Master’s degree or MBA is a plus).
* 5\\+ years of experience in Project Management, Program Management, or PMO roles (Project Management Office).
* Proven experience setting up or managing project structures in a cross\\-functional organization.
* PMP, PRINCE2, or equivalent project management certification.
* Strong analytical and organizational skills, with a structured and pragmatic working style.
* Excellent communication skills and confidence working with senior stakeholders.
* Experience using project management tools such as Jira, Asana, or Microsoft Project.
* Fluency in English and German.


**What We Offer**
* An appealing discount on all Yoshi en products.
* 28 days of paid time off per year plus up to 5 additional days off between Christmas and New Year.
* Urban Sports Club membership and a subsidy for the BVG public transport ticket.
* Various discounts of up to more than 50% on attractive brands such as Flaconi, Bosch, Apple or Expedia.
* Real ownership, plenty of room for new initiatives, and the opportunity to contribute your expertise and bring your ideas to life.
* Inviting office spaces with access to a wide range of organic teas and superfoods from around the world.
* We celebrate together: regular tea \\& office gatherings, summer festivities, and Christmas parties.


**About Us**
**As a multi\\-brand eCommerce group we are uniting brands such as
 **Yoshi en** 
 ,
 **EdenEve** 
 and
 **Dr. Schweikart** 
 , all driven by the principles of quality, fairness, innovation, and naturalness. Originating as sister companies of Sunday Natural and founded by Jörg Schweikart, these labels operate financially independent, each with its own distinct products and brand identities. Each brand is in diverse phases of business growth, possessing unique products and brand identities.**
****Yoshi en****
**, born out of a passion for tea, comprises more than 20 tea experts and professionals from over 10 countries, eager to share their knowledge and love for tea. Yoshi en thus sees itself not only as a provider of quality teas and accessories but also as an advocate for contemporary tea culture and wholesome living.**

title: Data Center Pre Development Project Manager
description:
**Data Center Construction \\- Pre\\-Development Project Manager – Germany** 




  





***flexibility on locations \\- Dortmund/Frankfurt/Berlin are the preferred locations \\- Regular travel will be required in Germany,***




  





**Global Data Center Business** 




  





 Global Data Center Business is seeking a Pre\\-Development Project Manager to lead and manage pre\\-development activities for our data center campus projects in Germany.
 



  





 This role is responsible for overseeing due diligence, entitlements, permitting, campus master planning, and budget development to enable predictable, on\\-time delivery of large\\-scale, mission\\-critical data center facilities. The Project Manager will collaborate daily with engineers, designers, development consultants, municipal and utility authorities, and internal stakeholders across Corporate Real Estate, Development, Engineering, Sales Engineering, Product, and Data Center Operations.
 



  





 The ideal candidate brings strong commercial real estate development expertise, executive\\-level communication skills, and the ability to navigate complex regulatory and permitting environments.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Manage due diligence, entitlements, and permitting for multiple projects across one or more data center campuses.
* Develop and maintain multi\\-phased campus master development plans, including comprehensive design solutions and capital budgets.
* Prepare and present monthly executive\\-level program updates to support informed decision\\-making.
* Identify and communicate entitlement and permitting risks; implement mitigation strategies to maintain project timelines and budgets.
* Establish and manage due diligence and permitting schedules, ensuring on\\-time completion through coordination of internal and external teams.
* Draft scopes of work for due diligence, master planning, and permitting; lead consultant/vendor evaluation, price leveling, selection, and contract negotiations.
* Monitor and control project budgets to ensure financial predictability and disciplined capital planning.
* Collaborate with Sales Engineering and Product teams to support custom client solutions within schedule and budget parameters.
* Represent Global Data Center Business in meetings with Authorities Having Jurisdiction (AHJs), regulatory agencies, economic development corporations, utilities, and public stakeholders.
* Continuously refine campus master plans to maintain accurate forecasting in a dynamic development environment.
* Contribute to the enhancement of internal development processes and project management standards.



  





**Basic Qualifications** 



* Five or more years of professional experience in commercial real estate development, property management, or land acquisition.
* Strong working knowledge of due diligence, zoning, entitlements, and permitting processes.
* Proficiency with Bluebeam, Smartsheet, Microsoft Office Suite (advanced Excel and PowerPoint required).
* Fluent in German; additional European language preferred.
* Ability to travel up to 25%.



  





**Preferred Qualifications** 



* MBA or Master’s degree in Engineering, Real Estate, Land Planning, Management, or related discipline.
* Experience managing a portfolio of projects across multiple markets.
* Experience delivering mission\\-critical data center facilities.
* Extensive experience in large\\-scale or complex land development projects.



  





**Knowledge, Skills \\& Abilities** 



* In\\-depth understanding of commercial real estate development processes.
* Strong familiarity with zoning regulations, environmental assessments, and municipal approval procedures.
* Knowledge of infrastructure planning, civil engineering fundamentals, and utility coordination.
* Experience in budget forecasting, financial modeling, and capital planning.
* Demonstrated success negotiating with regulatory agencies, consultants, vendors, and contractors.
* Ability to develop and deliver clear, executive\\-level presentations.
* Strong relationship\\-building skills with government entities and cross\\-functional stakeholders.
* Ability to operate effectively in high\\-pressure, deadline\\-driven environments.
* Strong analytical capability in interpreting complex regulatory and permitting requirements.



  





**Whats on offer:** 



* €120,000 \\- €150,000 Basic Salary
* Personal and Company Bonus @ 20% annually
* 25\\-32 Days Holiday
* Roth and Traditional 401(k) matching contributions with immediate vesting
* Every employee is bonus or commission eligible
* Generous PTO, Paid Volunteer Days Plus Floating Holidays
* Stock Purchase Plan (SPP)

title: Project Director (Germany)
description:
We are looking for an experienced Project Director to lead the delivery phase of a solar agri\\-PV energy projects, driving the project from the pre\\-construction stage through construction and handover to operations. The project’s connection date is expected in late 2028\\.
 



 We’re building our Germany team from the ground up, and you’ll be one of our first colleagues in the country. You’ll also become part of a diverse and international team based in the UK and Poland, collaborating closely across functions and projects.
 



 This is an exciting time for us, and we’re looking for a Project Director who would like to be part of this journey and built his own Project Manager team in near future.
 



 This role is on a hybrid basis, and our projects are located around Mecklenburg\\-Vorpommern. Frequent travel to the construction site is expected.
 



  





**What you’ll be doing:** 



* Lead the delivery of the Solar PV project within the agreed HSE objectives, timeline, budget, and quality standards.
* Implement company HSE policies and ensure all statutory requirements are fulfilled, including German regulations. Track and report on HSE performance.
* Drive effective health, safety, and wellbeing practices across the project team and contractors, and ensure the relevant safety management systems are in place.
* Manage the project end\\-to\\-end, from design and procurement through Construction, commissioning and handover to operations.
* Own Gülermak Renewables’ project management processes, including reporting to the steering committees.
* Manage internal and external stakeholders, including (but not limited to) local authorities, landowners, suppliers, owner’s engineers, technical and legal advisors, subcontractors, and internal functions such as commercial, across all project phases.
* Coordinate DNO and TNO connection works and ensure programme and interfaces alignment.
* Demonstrate a solid understanding of the commercial aspects of the Solar PV and Agri\\-PV market and technology.
* Collaborate with central function teams to monitor programme, budgets, design updates, and quality indicators, ensuring consistent project performance.
* Proactively manage project risks and opportunities by identifying, allocating, and controlling them, and implementing robust mitigation measures.
* Capture cost efficiencies during pre\\-construction phases without compromising project scope.
* Ensure effective communication and interface management with contractors and procurement functions.
* Coordinate the project handover process to the Asset Management function.
* Ensure efficient and effective close\\-out of all projects.



  





**What you’ll need:** 



* Proven and relevant track record as a Project Manager\\\\Director for grid\\-scale renewable energy construction projects.
* Project management experience across a range of disciplines, contracts, and procurement models.
* Experience with MV/HV works.
* Strong HSE focus, with a proven track record in managing German and EU HSE regulations in construction projects.
* Ability to manage a wide range of stakeholders and a team of support staff.
* Experience working with a systematic, risk\\-based management approach.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Ability to foster and promote collaborative working relationships.



**Desirable** 



* Experience working “client side” for utilities companies.
* Project management accreditation (e.g., PMI\\-PMP, APM).
* Experience managing Solar PV projects is highly desirable.



  





**Who we're:** 




 Gülermak Renewables is the renewable energy investment arm of Gülermak, a leading international infrastructure contractor with a 68\\-year track record of delivering complex projects to the highest standards of quality, ethics and execution.
 



 Headquartered in London, the renewables business operates as an independently managed platform, focused on the UK and Europe, with core investments in solar, onshore wind and battery energy storage, and a forward\\-looking view on technologies shaping the future energy system. With strong financial backing and deep infrastructure expertise, we build, own and operate renewable assets that deliver stable long\\-term cash flows. Our portfolio has reached c.650 MW to date, with clear ambition and strong momentum to scale materially in the near term.
 



 This role represents an exceptional opportunity to join a high\\-performing, entrepreneurial team comprising talented professionals with deep experience across renewables and infrastructure. You will have meaningful responsibility across the business, in a culture that rewards ownership, performance and merit.
 



 Our recruitment partner, NES Fircroft, is helping us find our future colleagues. Please reach out to Sarah if you have any questions or queries.
 



**Sarah.Hutter@nesfircroft.com or \\+49 621 3703 1976**

description:
We're working with a market and technology leader in professional radio technology for mission\\-critical infrastructure. This innovative company is expanding and seeking passionate talent to shape the future of professional radio communication.
